reasoning: The Lean statement captures each component of the informal statement precisely: `CompactSpace X` and `T2Space X` give "compact Hausdorff"; `IsClosed X1`, `IsClosed X2`, and `X1 ∪ X2 = univ` express that X is the union of two closed subspaces X1, X2; `MetrizableSpace X1` and `MetrizableSpace X2` (applied to `Set X`, which carries the subspace topology automatically) state that X1 and X2 are metrizable as subspaces; and the conclusion `MetrizableSpace X` matches "X is metrizable".
